query: CHP olağanüstü kurultay çağrı heyeti Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi başvuru karar - latest status as of late June / early July 2026. Has Özgür Özel's team filed the application under Civil Code Art 75 requesting appointment of a 3-person çağrı heyeti to convene a CHP extraordinary congress? Has any court issued a decision?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings on the CHP çağrı heyeti / Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi application status:

## Key Findings (as of latest reporting, 3 July 2026)

**Has Özel's team filed the çağrı heyeti application? — NO (not confirmed as filed).**
- MPs (notably Süleyman Bülbül) publicly announced they would file with the Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi on **Monday, 29 June 2026**, requesting appointment of a çağrı heyeti under Party Bylaws Art. 48 / the Civil Code Art. 75-type court mechanism.
- But the filing **did not proceed**. Özel's legal deputy **Gül Çiftçi (Odatv)** said: *"technical requirements are still forming... we will not yet make an application."*
- As of **3 July 2026**, it remained a **conditional threat**, not a completed filing.

**Has any court issued a decision on a çağrı heyeti? — NO.** No Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi decision exists because no application was formally adjudicated.

**Crucial context the question's framing partly inverts:** In this scenario Özel is **not** the sitting leader. A **21 May 2026 "mutlak butlan" ruling** (Ankara BAM 36th Civil Chamber) removed Özel and reinstated **Kılıçdaroğlu** via interim measure. It's Özel's *ousted* faction seeking the congress; the Kılıçdaroğlu-led Genel Merkez is blocking it, citing the tedbir kararı.

**Other status points:**
- 833 signatures delivered 17 June; Genel Merkez disputed them (820 → later only **99** claimed valid).
- Mutlak butlan file had **not yet reached the Yargıtay**; Özel's camp awaits a Yargıtay ruling before the **20 July adli tatil**.
- A separate related "clarification" application was **rejected by an İstinaf court on 26 June 2026**.
- New-party contingency increasingly discussed.

*Data vintage: latest source 3 July 2026; fast-moving situation.*

sources.title: CHP Genel Merkezi kurultay talebini reddetti, Özel çağrı heyeti için mahkeme yolunu işaret etti
sources.snippet: As of 3 July 2026: Genel Merkez rejected the congress request citing the interim order; Özel points to Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi for a çağrı heyeti but had not filed; mutlak butlan file not yet sent to Yargıtay; Genel Merkez claimed only 99 of 833 signatures valid.

sources.title: CHP'de gündem kurultay için 'çağrı heyeti'... Başvuru bugün denilmişti ama... Gül Çiftçi Odatv'ye anlattı
sources.snippet: Gül Çiftçi: 'Kurultay için teknik bazı hususlar oluşuyor. O aşamaya gelinmediği için henüz bir başvuru yapmayacağız.' — no filing yet.

sources.title: CHP'de mahkeme süreci başlıyor: Süleyman Bülbül'den 'çağrı heyeti' çıkışı
sources.snippet: Bülbül announced application to civil courts on Monday 29 June 2026 to request a çağrı heyeti, citing Bylaws Art. 48 and the 45-day congress requirement.

sources.title: Özgür Özel'den Kılıçdaroğlu'na çağrı: 'Partiyi bu ayıptan kurtarmalı'
sources.snippet: Explains CHP bylaws mechanism: application to sulh hukuk mahkemesi will be either rejected or accepted with appointment of a çağrı heyeti to hold congress within 45 days; Kılıçdaroğlu side says tedbir kararı bars any congress.

sources.title: CHP'de kurultay krizi büyüyor! Özel'in ekibi bugün mahkemeye gidiyor
sources.snippet: Özel team to apply to sulh hukuk mahkemesi for a çağrı heyeti; if accepted CHP goes to extraordinary congress within at most 45 days. References 21 May mutlak butlan ruling.

sources.title: İstinaf CHP için yapılan olağanüstü kurultay talebini reddetti
sources.snippet: 26 June 2026: appellate court rejected a related 'kararın açıklanması' application tied to the extraordinary congress dispute.

sources.title: Kurultay talebi yargı yolunda
sources.snippet: Details 833 signatures (from ~1,133 minus 170 İstanbul), plan to apply to sulh hukuk mahkemesi 29 June if congress not called; Genel Merkez says signatures won't be processed due to tedbir.

sources.title: CHP'de karar haftası
sources.snippet: 833 signatures delivered; 10-day deadline expired Friday (26 June); Kılıçdaroğlu administration cited 820 signatures under review, gave no formal response.

sources.title: CHP'de yeni parti için gözler 20 Temmuz'da, İmamoğlu'ndan 'parti' açıklaması
sources.snippet: Özel aides await a Yargıtay decision before the 20 July judicial recess; sulh hukuk mahkemesi application described as a possible step; new party option on the table.

sources.title: Kritik tarih 26 Haziran... Kılıçdaroğlu yönetimine tanınan süre doluyor
sources.snippet: 833 notarized signatures; deadline for processing expires 26 June; if steps not taken, congress request to be taken to judiciary.

key_facts: A 'mutlak butlan' (absolute nullity) ruling by Ankara Bölge Adliye Mahkemesi 36th Civil Chamber dated 21 May 2026 removed Özgür Özel and CHP organs from office and reinstated Kemal Kılıçdaroğlu as leader via interim measure; Özel remains parliamentary group chair.
Özel's faction collected 833 notarized delegate signatures for an extraordinary congress, delivered to CHP Genel Merkez on 17 June 2026 with a 10-day deadline.
The 10-day/tüzük deadline expired 26 June 2026 with no congress call; Genel Merkez cited the court's interim measure (tedbir kararı) as justification for refusal.
Genel Merkez disputed signature validity — first citing 820, later claiming only 99 of the 833 were valid (arguing many signatories were MPs/mayors, not eligible former delegates).

Özel-aligned MPs (notably Süleyman Bülbül) announced they would file with the Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi on Monday 29 June 2026 to request appointment of a çağrı heyeti, citing Party Bylaws Art. 48 and the Civil Code Art. 75-type court mechanism.
The application was NOT filed as announced: Özel's legal deputy Gül Çiftçi told Odatv 'certain technical requirements are still forming... we will not yet make an application.'
As of 3 July 2026, no çağrı heyeti application had been formally filed and no court had issued any decision on appointing a çağrı heyeti; it remained a conditional threat.
As of 3 July 2026, the mutlak butlan file had not yet been sent from the İstinaf court to the Yargıtay (Court of Cassation).
Özel's camp awaits a Yargıtay decision before the 20 July 2026 judicial recess (adli tatil), viewed as a critical threshold; a new-party option is increasingly discussed.
An İstinaf court rejected a related 'kararın açıklanması' (clarification) application on 26 June 2026.
Under CHP bylaws, a court-appointed çağrı heyeti, if granted, would convene the extraordinary congress within at most 45 days.

data_points.metric: Notarized delegate signatures submitted for extraordinary congress
Signatures Genel Merkez claimed were valid (contested)
Alternative valid-signature figure announced by Kılıçdaroğlu administration
Maximum days to hold extraordinary congress after çağrı heyeti/approval (per bylaws)
